primary_task: Identify relevant work and reach the author
success_condition: The reviewer can find role, scope, evidence, and contact without an unnecessary detour
evidence: before and after screenshots at 1440 and 390 CSS px

changes.reason: The cards repeated claims already stated in the verified project summaries and added no decision-relevant evidence.
The prior simplification removed the only visible focus cue.

checks.evidence: Removing the values cards did not remove a unique fact, task step, state, or contact path.
checks.impact: The page is shorter and preserves the hiring review task.
checks.verification: Compare the grounded fact inventory with the rendered page and repeat the primary task.

checks.evidence: Project copy contains named roles, dates, constraints, and outputs that do not fit an unrelated company.
checks.impact: The page retains a specific author identity.
checks.verification: Repeat the substitution test after copy changes.

checks.evidence: Heading and source order inspection shows identity, work index, project details, and contact in task order.
checks.impact: The structure remains understandable without decorative layout.
checks.verification: Inspect the accessibility tree and document outline after markup changes.

checks.evidence: Name, role, selected work, and contact remain distinct at a brief glance.
A reviewer can open a relevant project and reach contact in the declared path without blocked controls.
checks.verification: Run the documented task script on each release candidate.

checks.evidence: A fixture with 60 projects uses the same project-index record schema and pagination rule.
checks.impact: The archive can grow without new card families.
checks.remediation: Validate with production data when available.
checks.verification: Render the large fixture at supported viewports.

checks.evidence: At 320 CSS px equivalent, content stacks without loss or unintended horizontal scrolling.
checks.impact: Content remains available at the declared zoom and viewport baseline.
checks.verification: Repeat at 400% zoom in the supported desktop browser and at 320 CSS px.

checks.evidence: All links and controls are reachable in task order and focus remains visible and unobscured.
checks.impact: Keyboard users retain the primary path.
checks.verification: Repeat the keyboard script after navigation or overlay changes.

checks.evidence: Decorative entry movement is removed when reduced motion is enabled; state feedback remains.
checks.impact: The interface preserves information without unnecessary movement.
checks.verification: Exercise every motion pattern with the OS preference enabled.

checks.evidence: Each project claim maps to an approved project note; no customer metric or quote is displayed without a source.
checks.impact: The simplified page does not replace missing proof with fabricated proof.
checks.verification: Reconcile rendered claims against the content inventory before release.

checks.evidence: The shared project-card and image contours use a 20px outer radius and 12px inner radius across an 8px inset.
checks.impact: The simplified surfaces retain a coherent nested contour without adding radius variants.
checks.verification: Recompute the concentric offset and compare rendered corners at supported viewports.

checks.evidence: No screen-reader sampling was included in this example scope.
checks.impact: Some runtime announcements or navigation behavior may remain unverified.
checks.remediation: Add sampling when the project support matrix requires it.
checks.verification: Run the primary task with the declared screen-reader and browser combinations.

limitations: This example is a report contract demonstration, not evidence about a real site.
The non-required assistive-technology sample remains unknown.
notes: Passing this validator does not establish complete accessibility conformance or usability.
